reforms.description: The Greek government has initiated a substantial investment and modernization program for OTE, aiming to digitalize 70% of the telecommunications network by the end of 1998 and complete the process by 2000, with partial financing from EU funds.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The modernization of telecommunications infrastructure is expected to enhance connectivity and efficiency, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The program was initiated and completed as planned within the specified timeline.
reforms.importance_rationale: The investment and modernization program represents a significant structural change in the telecommunications sector.
reforms.source_quote: The current timetable is to digitalise 70 per cent of the network by end-1998, and complete the process by 2000.

reforms.description: The Greek government passed legislation in February 1998 requiring public enterprise management and unions in loss-making enterprises to agree on more flexible working conditions, with the possibility of imposing new conditions if no agreement is reached within six months.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By promoting flexible working conditions, the reform aims to enhance productivity and competitiveness in loss-making enterprises.
reforms.status_evidence: The legislation was passed and is currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The legislation introduces significant changes to labor relations in public enterprises, which can impact innovation and productivity.
reforms.source_quote: The Government passed legislation in February 1998 that requires public enterprise management and the respective unions in loss-making enterprises to agree to revise the existing collective agreements so as to achieve more flexible working conditions.
